task_completion.description: Agent opens the export dialog in the dive log application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export' dialog window or menu is visible on the screen within the dive log application.

task_completion.description: Agent selects the UDDF format for export.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'UDDF' (Universal Dive Data Format) option is explicitly selected, checked, or highlighted in the export dialog's format list.

task_completion.description: Agent specifies the correct save path and filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file save dialog shows the destination folder as '/home/ga/Documents' (or the Documents folder) and the filename entered is exactly 'exported_dives.uddf'.
The agent clicks the 'Save', 'Export', or 'OK' button in the file dialog to finalize the export process.

task_completion.description: The exported file is successfully created in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window displays 'exported_dives.uddf' existing inside the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the actual dive log application to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with a genuine dive log GUI (e.g., Subsurface) to trigger the export, rather than writing a script or manually typing XML.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't just create an empty or dummy file to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use terminal commands like 'touch exported_dives.uddf' or 'echo > exported_dives.uddf' to fake the output file.

integrity.description: Verifies the export process was genuinely executed through the application's native features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog and save prompts belong to the dive log application and match standard OS file dialogs, without the agent copy-pasting expected XML answers from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/exported_dives.uddf'. The exported UDDF file must be a valid XML containing at least 5 dives (the loaded file actually contains 29 dives) and be at least 1024 bytes in size.
